codeReview.summary: Clean implementation following established Effect service patterns. The MistralOcrService is well-structured with Context.GenericTag, Data.TaggedError, domain types (OcrResult, OcrUsage), and a live layer reading MISTRAL_API_KEY from Config. Uses @ai-sdk/mistral with pixtral-large-latest model via generateText for vision-based OCR. 8 tests cover happy paths, ConfigError, error wrapping, and live layer construction. All expected exports are in the barrel. AiToolkitLive already includes MistralOcrLive. One non-blocking code smell identified.

issues.description: Dead conditional branch: both sides of the ternary `input instanceof URL ? { type: 'file' as const, data: input, mediaType } : { type: 'file' as const, data: input, mediaType }` produce identical objects. The conditional is meaningless — the code works correctly but reads as if the developer intended to differentiate URL vs Buffer handling and forgot to do so. Should be simplified to a single object literal: `const filePart = { type: 'file' as const, data: input, mediaType };`.

ConfigError test only asserts `exit._tag === 'Failure'` without verifying the specific error type is ConfigError. While sufficient for basic coverage, a more precise assertion (e.g., checking Cause.squash produces a ConfigError) would make the test more robust and prevent false passes if the failure reason changes.

summary: The mistral-ocr-service feature passes review. The implementation correctly follows the established Effect service pattern (matching firecrawl.ts closely), exports all required types and layers, adds @ai-sdk/mistral to the pnpm catalog and package dependencies, and includes 8 well-structured tests. The service is already integrated into AiToolkitLive. Two non-blocking issues identified: a dead conditional branch in filePart construction and a loosely-typed ConfigError assertion in tests. Neither impacts correctness.
